page: Amazon Web Services c7a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The memory configuration consists of 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 5 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Text processing performance is highly efficient, ranking in the top 10% of Geekbench tests. Multi-core operations, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, align with average tier results. Memory bandwidth tests show a distinct tradeoff: while small-block cached reads and large-block writes perform at average levels, large-block uncached reads (256MB) fall into the bottom 25%, indicating potential bottlenecks for memory-intensive workloads that bypass the cache.

The instance is applicable for compute-bound workloads such as single-threaded processing, text processing, and lightweight language model inference, where its strong single-core execution and DDR5 memory speed are advantageous. However, the lack of local storage and the lower memory-to-CPU ratio make it less practical for large-scale database hosting or memory-intensive applications. The qualitative cost profile reflects a compute-optimized tier where users trade off high memory capacity and local storage for dedicated, high-frequency CPU execution.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier text processing capabilities, though multi-core execution and large-block uncached memory reads perform at average to weak levels. This hardware profile is aligned with compute-bound tasks, single-threaded applications, and text processing workloads that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation, while presenting a tradeoff for memory-intensive or storage-dependent operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance for text and compute-bound processing.

tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized configuration featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and DDR5 memory for single-threaded and text-processing workloads.

16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (2.0 GB per core)
Strong single-core CPU and top-tier text processing benchmark performance
3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
